definitions: To make a painful situation worse by reminding someone of a past hurt or by adding to their current distress.
To exacerbate a recent emotional injury or grievance.
examples: Bringing up his ex-girlfriend was like pouring salt on an all-too-fresh wound.
Her insensitive comment was just pouring salt on an all-too-fresh wound after his job loss.
Don't mention the failed project; it's pouring salt on an all-too-fresh wound for the team.
lemma: pour salt on an all-too-fresh wound
source: pouring salt on an all-too-fresh wound
